headline: RM40 Billion in Student Loans Nobody Knows How to Collect
context: PTPTN's outstanding loans reached RM40.2B with a repayment rate of 47%, while the government proposed converting to income-contingent repayment without a functional tax integration system.

cards.big: RM40 billion lent. RM19 billion repaid. Nobody has a plan.
cards.sub: The repayment crisis has been reported every year since 2010. The number only grows.

cards.big: 47% repayment rate. But 38% of borrowers earn below RM2,000.
cards.sub: They are not refusing to pay. They cannot pay. The loan assumes salaries that do not exist.

cards.big: LHDN integration needed for income-contingent repayment. Timeline: unknown.
cards.sub: IRB and PTPTN systems are incompatible. The data-sharing framework has no legislation.

cards.big: Loan default triggers travel bans. 420,000 Malaysians currently blacklisted.
cards.sub: Punishing graduates who earn poverty wages for debts incurred at 18.

cards.big: Is this a loan crisis or a graduate salary crisis?
cards.sub: If the economy paid what degrees promise, repayment would not be a problem.

cards.big: PTPTN is downstream of a labour market that undervalues graduates. Fixing collection without fixing wages solves nothing.
